text: delegate codex lanes failed twice mid-build (work-account quota, then an expired restored token) but both surfaced as generic harness_error; a quota/auth-specific failure reason would have saved ~30 minutes of diagnosis

exec_command returned no captured output for successful multi-line inventory commands, forcing repeated simpler probes; preserving output or surfacing why it was dropped would avoid redundant diagnostics

Python re.sub treated backslashes in verbatim paper-cut text as replacement escapes during Markdown report generation, causing a bad-escape retry; use a function replacement whenever inserting untrusted text
Broad hook searches traverse historical JSON/session backups; provide a live-hook-only discovery helper or documented excludes to avoid noisy and potentially sensitive output.

Codex exposes environment-inheritance controls but no documented shell-command selection knob; a parent SHELL/BASH_ENV codex exec probe did not yield the required bash/GNU-tool identity. Add a supported per-harness shell command setting or diagnostic.
Homebrew Bash printf treats a leading hyphen format as an option; use printf "%s\\n" for separator strings in shared shell diagnostics.
A Bash regression probe compared ${!array[@]} inside test brackets, where separate expansion words cause a false too-many-arguments failure; use the scalar ${!array[*]} form for equality assertions.
The Wave 1 GNU awk regression fixture separated x and y across lines while testing /x(y)/; use a same-line xy fixture so the capture-array assertion tests GNU awk rather than failing the fixture.
Wave 1 completion report named the retained backup directory but omitted its internal filenames, causing a failed review-bundle copy; report the exact backup paths or a directory listing.
Audit commands that print sourced credential files can expose secret values; use names/booleans-only inspection helpers.
Scratch sed portability test assumed /bin/sed; macOS provides /usr/bin/sed.
The final diff redaction helper used a non-portable sed regex and failed; inspect structured JSONL diffs without regex masking.
Filtering JSONL additions from git diff with a broad plus-line match included the +++ header and broke jq; use a strict '^+{' filter before parsing.
ShellCheck reports pre-existing SC1091 source-following and SC2155 export-assignment warnings in agent-shell/env.sh, so scoped verification needs explicit known-warning exclusions.
zsh reserves the common test-harness variable name status, causing a shell test to fail before its assertions; a shell-safe harness example would prevent this.
A wrapper-header inspection followed the claude symlink into a Mach-O binary and dumped binary bytes; detect file type before sed/head and inspect only text wrappers.
zsh reserves status as a read-only special parameter, so portable verification snippets should use a neutral exit-code variable such as rc.
cargo test accepts one filter per invocation; use separate targeted runs or one module filter
clippy rejected a nonminimal boolean in the evidence redactor; simplify boundary predicates before the final gate
Test assertion accidentally used serde_json::to_value Result directly; use unwrap before as_object.
cargo test accepts only one positional test filter; a focused multi-test invocation fails before running tests
cargo test accepts one name filter, so multiple focused tests require separate invocations

text: Installed papercuts binary rejects multi-ID resolve and evidence flags although this checkout's Wave 2 contract requires them; use this checkout's release binary for remediation.
Wave 3 manifest gate requires explicit Claude accept and Codex defer harness outcomes; acceptance artifact should preserve the exact invocation
Radar docs-only commit hook is blocked by pre-existing AskMarkdown typecheck errors: missing react-markdown and implicit-any link renderer bindings
Delegate CLI has no ps subcommand despite being the obvious way to discover handles for concurrently launched runs; a documented list/status command would prevent blind polling.

delegate CLI: --prompt-file /dev/stdin combined with piped stdin fails as ambiguous_prompt_source; either special-case /dev/stdin or document that stdin heredocs must not use --prompt-file (delegate grok work, exit 2)

Claude Code persistent Bash-tool shell runs Homebrew bash with BASH_ENV set in env, but agent.sh was not sourced: PATH lacks gnubin so sed/awk resolve to BSD /usr/bin. Same file works under a fresh 'bash -c'. Wave 1 acceptance shape (bash -c) passes; the persistent-shell shape needs its own acceptance row or a login-shell profile hook.
Skill reconciliation inventory used macOS stat flags even though the agent shell resolves GNU stat; a shared portable mtime helper would have prevented the failed pass.

stat: cannot read file system information for 'mtime=%Sm epoch=%m files=': No such file or directory
stat: cannot read file system information for '%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S': No such file or directory

text: Wave 4b coordinator context identified installed exa-agent as 0.3.0, but the live binary reports 0.2.0; live-version evidence should accompany staged-skill handoffs.
The remediation plan requires an upstream issue draft under ignored _scratch/, so the normal git add checkpoint failed; the plan should explicitly say to force-add this named artifact.

text: A second collaboration.spawn_agent call with fork_turns=all failed because the current thread id was not found; a stable retry path or clearer fork-context error would prevent review-lane setup friction.

evidence.stderr: collab spawn failed: no thread with id: <redacted>

text: post mail: replying to a sender is a dead end unless their room is pre-registered — 'post rooms' shows registered rooms but there is no 'post rooms add' command; registration means hand-editing ~/.claude-mail/rooms.json, whose flat name->path shape is undocumented in --help (first guess produced an invalid nested shape). A 'post rooms add <name> <path>' subcommand, or auto-suggesting registration when a reply targets a known sender, would have prevented this.

A report-section extraction loop embedded Markdown backticks in a double-quoted regex, so zsh executed each cut ID and later rejected the evidence command itself; a shared literal-heading lookup recipe would prevent these retries.

evidence.cmd: report heading extraction loop with Markdown backticks
evidence.stderr: zsh parsed Markdown backticks as command substitution and rejected the constructed argument.

text: Wave 8 manifest gating depends on three retained Delegate worktree ledgers that were later removed, dropping frozen-snapshot coverage from 132/132 to 128/132; a durable canonical aggregate or protected evidence copy would prevent remediation from becoming unverifiable after worktree cleanup.

text: A cross-repo verification grep assumed exa-agent-cli had a scripts directory and added a noisy missing-path error; checking candidate paths before passing them to rg would keep forensic output clean.

text: The deletion-safety hook blocked a read-only guidance verification because the forbidden command name appeared only inside a quoted rg pattern; token-aware command parsing would preserve the guard without blocking documentation checks.

evidence.stderr: Command blocked by PreToolUse hook because the read-only rg pattern contained the blocked deletion token inside quotes.

text: A bulk draft generator duplicated the Markdown extension because file mappings and writer logic both owned the suffix; one canonical filename owner or a suffix assertion would prevent the cleanup pass.

text: A Wave 8 continuation probe guessed a dated manifest filename before finding the repo's undated canonical path; naming the exact manifest path in continuation handoffs would avoid the noisy dead end.

text: delegate work-mode auto-isolation flipped to worktree mid-day (Wave 6 in-flight edits to the live tool): three review lanes failed fast with dirty_source_workspace on repos whose only dirt was untracked ledgers/scratch. Per Trey's ratified ruling work mode should stay in-place by default; if worktree stays auto, untracked-only dirt needs a gentler path than hard refusal.
